functions.name: mm_std.date.utc_random
documentation: def parse_date(value: str, ignore_tz: bool = False) -> datetime:

functions.name: mm_std.str.str_starts_with_any
documentation: check if str starts with any of prefixes

functions.name: mm_std.str.str_ends_with_any
documentation: check if str ends with any of prefixes

methods.name: mm_std.result.Ok.map_or_else
documentation:
The contained result is `Ok`, so return original value mapped to
a new value using the passed in `op` function.

methods.name: mm_std.result.Ok.map_err
documentation: The contained result is `Ok`, so return `Ok` with the original value

methods.name: mm_std.result.Ok.and_then
documentation:
The contained result is `Ok`, so return the result of `op` with the
original value passed in. If return of `op` function is not Result, it will be a Ok value.

methods.name: mm_std.result.Err.unwrap_or_else
documentation:
The contained result is `Err`, so return the result of applying
`op` to the error value.

methods.name: mm_std.result.Err.unwrap_or_raise
documentation: The contained result is `Err`, so raise the exception with the value.

methods.name: mm_std.result.Err.map_or_else
documentation: Return the result of the default operation

methods.name: mm_std.result.Err.and_then
documentation: The contained result is `Err`, so return `Err` with the original value
